method_atom: A classification-and-counting framework for a single binary operation based on the symmetric relations (identities) it satisfies, where each such relation is a word equation of length 2k using the same k variables once on each side. The method organizes these relations into equivalence classes ordered by logical implication (which relations force others, such as commutativity or associativity in long products), and introduces a functorial reduction that associates a permutation/wreath-product construction with each relation, transferring the questions into group theory so they can be settled by automated computational tools (finite model builders and automated theorem provers). Labelled tree combinatorics enumerate the parenthesizations.
object_atom: Groupoids (sets equipped with one binary multiplication) and the varieties they define, together with labelled binary trees encoding products of factors and Boolean algebras as a concrete equational system.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the identity-classification/counting plus the wreath-product reduction to group theory attacked by automated provers; the domain-specific object is the algebraic universe of groupoids, binary trees, and Boolean algebras.
